words.definition: to make use of a situation or weakness
in a way that is tempting but out of reach
scattered pieces of waste or remains
doubt about whether something will work
showing off or bragging about achievements
things that block progress or make something difficult

questions.question: What was the crow searching for on the hot summer day?
options: Food to eat
Water to quench his thirst
A place to build a nest

questions.question: What problem did the crow discover with the pitcher?
options: It was completely empty
The water was too low for his beak to reach
It was too heavy to move

questions.question: What did the crow try to do to reach the water?
options: He tried to break the pitcher
He tried to stretch his neck and tip the pitcher over
He tried to call for help

questions.question: What was the crow known for throughout the forest?
options: His beautiful singing voice
His intelligence and problem-solving abilities
His ability to fly very fast

questions.question: What did the crow notice around the base of the oak tree?
options: Other birds and animals
Small stones and pebbles scattered everywhere
Food and water

questions.question: What was the crow's brilliant idea?
options: To find a different pitcher
To drop stones in the pitcher to make the water rise
To break the pitcher with rocks

questions.question: What happened each time the crow dropped a stone in the pitcher?
options: Nothing changed
The water level rose just a tiny bit
The pitcher cracked

questions.question: How did the crow respond to the other animals' doubts?
options: He gave up and went home
He paid no attention and continued working
He argued with them

questions.question: What happened as more stones accumulated in the pitcher?
options: The pitcher became too heavy
The water level began to rise more noticeably
The stones broke the pitcher

questions.question: Why did the water taste so delicious to the crow?
options: Because it was magical water
Because he had earned it through cleverness and hard work
Because it came from a special well

questions.question: What did the crow teach the other animals about problem-solving?
options: That problems are always impossible to solve
That creative thinking can find ways around obstacles
That only crows are smart enough to solve problems

questions.question: What lesson did the crow's story teach throughout the forest?
options: That water is always easy to find
That determination, patience, and creative thinking can solve impossible problems
That stones are more valuable than water

informationalTexts.title: Glaciers
informationalTexts.content:
A glacier is a massive sheet of ice that forms when snow accumulates for many years without completely melting. As more snow piles up, the weight compresses the lower layers into dense ice. Over thousands of years, these ice sheets can become hundreds of feet thick and begin to move slowly downhill due to gravity, like very slow rivers of ice.
Glaciers are incredibly powerful forces that shape the landscape around them. As they move, they carve deep valleys, create lakes, and transport huge boulders for miles. Today, most of the world's glaciers are found in places like Antarctica, Greenland, and high mountain ranges. Scientists study glaciers carefully because they contain important information about Earth's climate history and are sensitive indicators of climate change.

questions.question: How do glaciers form?
options: snow accumulates for many years and gets compressed into ice
rivers freeze completely
rain turns into ice

questions.question: How do glaciers change the landscape?
options: they carve valleys, create lakes, and move boulders
they make the ground flat
they only melt and disappear

informationalTexts.title: Engineers
informationalTexts.content:
Engineers are problem-solvers who design and build things that make our lives better and safer. There are many different types of engineers: civil engineers design roads and bridges, electrical engineers work with power systems and electronics, mechanical engineers create machines and engines, and aerospace engineers develop airplanes and spacecraft. All engineers use math, science, and creativity to solve complex challenges.
To become an engineer, a person must study mathematics and science extensively in college. Engineers must think carefully about safety, cost, and how their designs will affect people and the environment. They often work in teams, testing their ideas multiple times before building the final product. From the smartphones we use to the buildings we live in, engineers have helped create almost everything in our modern world.

questions.question: What do all types of engineers have in common?
options: they use math, science, and creativity to solve problems
they only work with computers
they only build bridges

questions.question: What must engineers consider when designing?
options: safety, cost, and environmental impact
only how much money they can make
just what looks pretty

informationalTexts.title: Coral Reefs
informationalTexts.content:
Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems built by tiny marine animals called coral polyps. These small creatures create hard, colorful skeletons from calcium carbonate, and over thousands of years, millions of these skeletons build up to form large reef structures. Coral reefs are found in warm, shallow ocean waters and are often called "rainforests of the sea" because they support incredible biodiversity.
Thousands of different species call coral reefs home, including colorful fish, sea turtles, sharks, and countless invertebrates. The reefs provide food, shelter, and breeding grounds for these animals. Coral reefs also protect coastlines from storms and waves, and provide food and income for millions of people worldwide. Unfortunately, pollution, warming ocean temperatures, and other human activities are threatening coral reefs around the globe.

questions.question: What builds coral reefs?
options: tiny marine animals called coral polyps
large fish
underwater plants

questions.question: Why are coral reefs important?
options: they support biodiversity and protect coastlines
they are pretty to look at
they make the water warmer

informationalTexts.title: Architects
informationalTexts.content:
Architects are professionals who design buildings and other structures where people live, work, and play. They create detailed plans that show how buildings should look and function, considering factors like safety, beauty, cost, and how the building will be used. Architects must understand engineering principles, local building codes, and environmental concerns when creating their designs.
Before any construction begins, architects work closely with their clients to understand their needs and preferences. They create drawings, blueprints, and sometimes even computer models to show what the finished building will look like. Architects also coordinate with engineers, contractors, and other specialists throughout the building process to ensure that their vision becomes reality safely and efficiently.

questions.question: What factors must architects consider when designing?
options: safety, beauty, cost, and how the building will be used
only what looks nice
just the color of the building

questions.question: Who do architects work with during the building process?
options: engineers, contractors, and other specialists
only their clients

nobody else

informationalTexts.content:

2. b) go to school for many years to learn about animal care

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
